title_en: Brominated Vegetable Oil: Why Is It Still In Our Drinks?
title_es: Aceite Vegetal Bromado: ¿Por Qué Sigue en Nuestras Bebidas?
hook_en: Did you know your favorite soda might contain a banned chemical?
hook_es: ¿Sabías que tu refresco favorito podría tener un químico prohibido?
key_message: Brominated Vegetable Oil is banned in many countries due to health risks, yet it's still in some US beverages.
visual_elements: map showing countries where BVO is banned
graphic of stats on health risks
_source_regulation_context:
ADDITIVES BANNED IN OTHER COUNTRIES BUT LEGAL IN US:
  • Brominated Vegetable Oil (BVO): Sodas, sports drinks (emulsifier for citrus flavoring) — Banned in: EU, Japan, India — Health concern: Bromine accumulates in body tissue; linked to neurological and thyroid issues. FDA proposed ban in 2023

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_en: Obesity and Deregulation: A Timeline You Need to See
title_es: Obesidad y Desregulación: Una Línea de Tiempo Que Debes Ver
hook_en: Watch how obesity has skyrocketed as regulations loosened!
hook_es: ¡Mira cómo la obesidad se disparó al aflojar las regulaciones!
key_message: The increase in obesity rates correlates with deregulation and the rise of ultra-processed foods.
visual_elements: animated arrows linking deregulation and obesity rates
